intro: Strobilanthes dispar is a plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae, commonly known as the 'Blue Pilea' due to its striking blue flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in the Indian subcontinent, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Strobilanthes dispar include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
